data.title: Wall Street craters a day after historic rally, S&P 500 threatens trading halt
data.description:
Wall Street's euphoria disappeared on Thursday as market participants were back in selloff mode. More than half of the previous session's historic advance was wiped out.

The three major averages steadily fell until just after noon, following which they recouped some losses. The benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) even threatened a stock circuit breaker. It was last -5.12% at 5,177.54 points, after falling as much as -6.26%. 

The tech-heavy Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) was -5.91% at 16,113.25 points. The blue-chip Dow (DJI) was -4.28% at 38,870.83 points.  

On Wednesday, U.S. President Donald Trump's announcement of a 90-day pause on most reciprocal tariffs except for those against China sent the S&P (SP500) to its best day since the 2008 financial crisis. The Nasdaq (COMP:IND) logged its second-best day ever. 

But traders on Thursday came to grips with the fact that, even with the 90-day pause, it was just a short-term relief from trade war escalation and that longer-term recession risks were still very much real.

"Even with the 90-day postponement of the punitive tariffs we retain our call that the U.S. economy will slip into a modest recession that will look like a garden variety inventory correction that will last between 6 to 9 months. There are simply too many simultaneous shocks that are moving through the economy for it to slip the surly bonds of recessions gravity," Joseph Brusuelas, principal and chief economist at RSM US LLP, said on X (formerly Twitter).

The White House confirmed that the tariff rate against China is now actually 145%, not 125% as conveyed on Wednesday. The continued trade war escalation between the world's two biggest economies stoked concerns.

"The stock market is taking a breather after Wednesday's outlandish rally, as investors start to realize that even with the pause, a trade war with China is still happening, with very little clarity on the path ahead between the two nations.  It is common after big up days to see a pullback. The market is trying to find its footing after a week of whiplash," Robert Ruggirello, chief investment officer at Brave Eagle Wealth Management, said. 

Even a downside surprise in a key inflation gauge did little to brighten the mood. Before the opening bell, data from the U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics showed a -0.1% M/M decrease in the March consumer price index (CPI), compared to an expected figure of +0.1%. Core CPI came in at +0.1% M/M, lower than the +0.3% consensus. 

However, economists see the March print as the final reading before the effects of tariffs start showing up.   

"No obvious evidence of tariffs in the March numbers even though China 20% and some others already in effect then. But many more tariffs started in April and some have lag to prices. So expect inflation to pick up in the coming months," Jason Furman, former deputy director of the U.S. National Economic Council, said on X.   

Over in the fixed-income markets, a rally in bonds extended to a second straight day. The 10-year yield (US10Y) was last up 7 basis points to 4.37%. The shorter-end more rate-sensitive 2-year yield (US2Y) was up 20 basis points to 3.94%.  

For more, see how Treasury yields have done across the curve on the Seeking Alpha bond page.

data.title: White House clarifies tariff on China is 145% due to fentanyl levy: report
data.description:
The White House on Thursday clarified that the cumulative tariff on China-produced goods coming into the US will total 145%, CNBC reported, citing an administration official.  

CNBC said the executive order issued Wednesday amended the reciprocal rate against China to 125% from 84% and that was on top of the 20% tariff over fentanyl, according to the official. 

Trump on Wednesday said he issued a 90-day pause on reciprocal tariffs on more than 75 countries – excluding China – that the White House reached out to for negotiations. 

Earlier Thursday, National Economic Council Director Kevin Hassett told CNBC close to 20 countries had reached out to White House to negotiate tariff deals.

data.title: SA analyst downgrades: JBLU, CB, NLY
data.description:
A round-up of the most recent downgrades by Seeking Alpha analysts.

JetBlue Airways (JBLU). Buy to hold by Stone Fox Capital. "JetBlue Airways should follow Delta Air Lines in lowering earnings estimates when reporting Q1 results in a few weeks…The stock should be watched from the sidelines due to the failure of the company to turn around the business heading into a potential recession."

Chubb (CB). Buy to hold by Seeking Profits. "The insurance sector is largely directly immune to tariffs, but Chubb's significant China presence poses indirect risks, potentially reducing its fair value by 1-2%...Current fair value of $289 suggests a 6.5-7% total return, insufficient for a buy rating, but I am maintaining a hold due to manageable tariff impact."

Annaly Capital Management (NYSE:NLY). Buy to sell by Sensor Unlimited. "The risk/return profile for NLY stock has shifted materially since my last writing. The shift is a result of a collection of new catalysts and developments, with the inversion of the yield curve being the most crucial one in my mind."

data.title: Could a pullback on AI capex for the mega-cap tech stocks be a positive? – Analyst
data.description:
There are opportunities over the long-term within mega-cap technology stocks, especially if there is pullback on capital expenditures on AI, according to Brave Eagle Wealth’s CIO.

“If we see mega-cap tech pullback on capex spend, that may actually be received as a positive, as investors just a few months ago were raising questions about big tech's extreme spending on AI,” said Robert Ruggirello, chief investment officer at Brave Eagle Wealth Management.

However, Alphabet’s (GOOGL), (GOOG) CEO has recently pledge to invest about $75B to build data center infrastructure. In the fourth quarter of 2024, the company spent $14B to help growth its business across Google Services, Google Cloud, and Google DeepMind.

Alphabet (GOOG) (GOOGL), Amazon (AMZN), Meta Platforms (META) and Microsoft (MSFT) are projected to boost their capex spending to $322B, up about 40% what was expected this year.  

The following are the biggest technology companies with their Seeking Alpha’s Quant rating and their CAPEX Growth year-over-year percentages.

Out of the top 10 stocks, only Adobe (ADBE) is “buy-rated” by Quant. Also, most companies are down year-to-date.

Seeking Alpha's Quant Rating system awards grades to stocks based on key quantitative measures, like valuation, growth and profitability.

  • Apple (AAPL) – Quant rating: 3.38; -25.53% YTD; CAPEX Growth YoY: 4.51%
  • Microsoft (MSFT) – Quant rating: 3.47; -12.03% YTD; CAPEX Growth YoY: 57.81%
  • Nvidia (NVDA) – Quant rating: 3.46; -23% YTD; CAPEX Growth YoY: 202.71%
  • Broadcom (AVGO) – Quant rating: 3.48; -27.61% YTD; CAPEX Growth YoY: 11.68%
  • Cisco Systems (CSCO) – Quant rating: 3.47; -6.54% YTD; CAPEX Growth YoY: -1.73%
  • International Business Machines (IBM) – Quant rating: 3.36; 1.66% YTD; CAPEX Growth YoY: -15.82%
  • Palantir Technologies (PLTR) – Quant rating: 3.49; 11.6% YTD; CAPEX Growth YoY: -16.41%
  • Intuit (INTU) – Quant rating: 3.35; -10.28% YTD; CAPEX Growth YoY: -52%
  • QUALCOMM (QCOM) – Quant rating: 3.42; -15.21% YTD; CAPEX Growth YoY: -12.8%
  • Adobe (ADBE) – Quant rating: 3.94; -22.53% YTD; CAPEX Growth YoY: -41.89%

data.title: Equinor forms new power unit to focus on rising data center demand
data.description:
Equinor (NYSE:EQNR) unveiled plans Thursday to create a new business to capitalize on the surging demand for power due to the expansion of artificial intelligence, data centers, and the green transition, even as it will continue to obtain the vast majority of its income from oil and gas.

The company said it will merge its renewable energy business with its gas-to-power plants and energy storage assets to boost its electricity business, and appointed Helge Haugane, head of gas and power in its marketing, midstream and processing business, as head of the new unit starting in September, when the organizational changes take effect.

Equinor (NYSE:EQNR) has built a sizable renewables business over the past 20 years, with offshore and onshore wind as well as solar, and it has three large offshore wind projects underway in the U.K., U.S. and Poland and an increasing number of onshore renewable assets, while also adding gas power plants and energy storage assets to provide flexible power and support intermittent wind and solar.

"While the demand for electricity from renewable power will continue to grow, flexible power will ensure reliability and stability in the power offering to the market," the company said.

data.title: Tariffs are a 'stagflationary shock,' Chicago Fed's Goolsbee said
data.description:
A tariff is a "stagflationary shock," which means it hurts both sides of the Federal Reserve's dual mandate of price stability and maximum employment, Chicago Fed President Austan Goolsbee said Thursday.

"There's no playbook" for the Fed to respond to a stagflationary shock, he added.

Still, he believes that interest rates will decline over the next one to two years, but the economy will have to get through some bumps between now and then, he added.

"The Fed timetable is not the market timetable, and the stock market is not the economy, " Goolsbee said in opening remarks at a moderated conversation on Thursday.

"Our goal is to find the through-line," not to jump to conclusions, he said during an event at the Economic Club of New York. It appears that the stock market "has jumped to conclusions" ahead of everyone else. 

Meanwhile, the hard data still "looks pretty good," he said.

He discussed the reverberations of tariffs that can build on the effects of the tariffs on prices, such as how much of the tariffs producers pass through to users and the retaliatory tariffs imposed by other countries. 

Developing... check back for updates.

data.title: Summit Therapeutics spikes after co-CEO’s early warrants exercise
data.description:
Shares of Summit Therapeutics (NASDAQ:SMMT) traded higher for the second straight session on Thursday after Cantor Fitzgerald said that an earlier-than-expected warrant exercise by the company’s co-CEO, Bob Duggan, indicated a bullish signal for the stock.

According to a regulatory filing on Wednesday, Duggan, a venture capitalist who already owns 556M shares of the company (70% of ownership), exercised warrants to buy nearly 4.0M SMMT shares about four years before their expiration.

Another regulatory filing indicated that Summit’s (NASDAQ:SMMT) co-CEO and board president, Maky Zanganeh, procured a similar number of SMMT shares after exercising her warrants roughly four years in advance.

“In our view, there is only one reason to exercise these warrants early. Bob thinks SMMT shares will go higher,” Cantor analyst Eric Schmidt wrote with an Overweight rating on the stock.

Both transactions took place on Tuesday as the company prepares to release overall survival (OS) data from its partner Akeso’s (OTCPK:AKESF) HARMONi-2 Phase 3 trial for its lead asset, ivonescimab.

The PD-1/VEGF bispecific antibody has already outperformed Merck’s (MRK) blockbuster cancer therapy Keytruda (pembrolizumab) against lung cancer, according to data on progression-free survival (PFS) released from the China-based study last year.

“Much is riding on whether ivonescimab is superior to pembro in OS, given we have already seen very strong PFS data from this study,” Schmidt added.

data.title: Long-term mortgage rates remain below 7% for twelfth straight week
data.description:
Long-term mortgage rates continued their downward trajectory, remaining under 7% for the twelfth consecutive week, the latest Freddie Mac Primary Mortgage Survey showed.

30-year fixed-rate mortgages averaged 6.62% as of April 10, down from 6.64% last week and 6.88% in the same period a year ago.

15-year fixed-rate mortgages averaged 5.82%, unchanged from last week and lower than 6.16% a year ago.

"As purchase applications continue to climb, the spring homebuying season is shaping up to look more favorable than last year," said Freddie Mac's chief economist, Sam Khater.

data.title: Amazon's Andy Jassy on TikTok U.S. bid: 'I don’t think we said that'
data.description:
Amazon's (NASDAQ:AMZN) top boss, Andy Jassy, said in an interview on CNBC Wednesday that the company did not say that it is not bidding for TikTok U.S.

The CEO of the e-commerce and tech giant did not comment on M&A rumors when asked further during the CNBC interview.

The statement by Jassy comes after several media reports from earlier this month that claimed that the company submitted a last-minute bid for the short video content app.

But when asked if TikTok is an important piece in the ongoing tariff conversation, Jassy said, "I don’t know... I think the conversations that the administration is having, probably was having before and more intensely is having now with each country are complicated. And I imagine that each have their set of variables and I’ve read that this may be one of the pieces in that negotiation, but I am not privy to it."

Jassy's statement comes after U.S. President Donald Trump further extended the deadline for the app by 75 days to keep it operational against the sale-or-ban law taking effect, which was passed under the Biden administration.

A Beijing spokesperson said Wednesday that any deal for TikTok U.S. has to be in compliance with Chinese law. China also indicated it would not approve any deal under Trump's reciprocal tariff threat.

data.title: SA analyst upgrades: TSLA, AAPL, NVDA, META, PLTR, ABBV, HUM, TGT
data.description:
A roundup of the most recent upgrades issued by Seeking Alpha analysts.

Tesla (NASDAQ:TSLA). Sell to hold by James Foord. "I recommend waiting for the next quarter's results and potential tariff resolutions before increasing TSLA stock holdings, targeting technical support levels around $160 or $100."

Tesla (NASDAQ:TSLA).  Sell to hold by Gary Alexander. "Tesla could prove an opportunistic buy now if the new Model Q vehicles, upon launch in mid-2025 (at least per the company's current official statement, though many are now expecting a delay) help to shift the conversation away from Musk's political activities and onto the cheaper product itself."

Nvidia (NASDAQ:NVDA). Hold to buy by Bay Area Ideas. "I upgrade Nvidia stock to a buy rating as the technical weakness and undervaluation may be a buying opportunity even though near-term declines may persist."

Nvidia (NASDAQ:NVDA). Sell to buy by MMMT Wealth.

Nvidia (NVDA). Hold to buy by Livy Investment Research.

Apple. (NASDAQ:AAPL). Sell to hold by Jonathan Weber. "Despite a recent valuation decline, AAPL is still not a bargain, trading at an earnings multiple of around 27, which is not especially attractive. Tariffs and trade tensions, particularly with China, pose substantial risks to the Company's supply chain and market demand, potentially impacting profitability."

Meta. (NASDAQ:META). Hold to buy by Dair Sansyzbayev.

AbbVie (NYSE:ABBV). Hold to buy by Stephen Ayers. "DCF analysis shows AbbVie is approximately 10% undervalued with a favorable risk/reward profile and an attractive 3.73% dividend yield."

Humana (NYSE:HUM). Sell to hold by Harrison Schwartz. "Humana is undervalued in the short term, but its long-term profitability is uncertain due to potential future cuts in Medicare Advantage spending and due to the rising fiscal deficit."

Palantir (NASDAQ:PLTR). Sell to hold by Deep Value Investing.

Target (NYSE:TGT). Hold to buy by Ken Taylor. "Target stock is undervalued, presenting a strong buy opportunity with the potential for 12.8% to 24.6% annualized total returns if management hits its targets."

Target (NYSE:TGT). Hold to buy by Amrita Roy. "I am upgrading Target stock to a "buy" with a price target of $115, representing a 31% upside, due to attractive risk-reward dynamics that have been created from the latest sell-off."

Coinbase (NASDAQ:COIN). Hold to buy by Mike Zaccardi.

Coinbase (NASDAQ:COIN). Hold to buy by Julian Lin.

Coinbase (COIN). Hold to buy by Michael Fay.

Albermarle (NYSE:ALB). Hold to buy by Juxtaposed Ideas.

MongoDB (NASDAQ:MDB). Hold to buy by Gary Alexander.

Elastic (NYSE:ESTC). Hold to buy by Eleceed Capital.

Constellation Energy (NASDAQ:CEG). Hold to buy by Laura Starks.
